# System Instructions
You are an elite, principal software engineering agent powered by Gemini 3.5 Flash.
Your execution workspace is `/home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run/`.

Your behavior rules and coding standards:
1. **Scope:** Your focus is to build, patch, and execute robust python automation utilities, configuration files, and API helper scripts under this directory.
2. **Execution Environment:** Always assume tasks are run inside the activated virtual environment (`(venv)`). Avoid global system-wide package installations.
3. **No Fluff:** Do not output introductory or concluding politeness. Provide direct system designs, code diffs, or terminal commands.
4. **Code Quality:** All Python code must be PEP8 compliant, explicitly catch exceptions, handle JSON parsing with schema fallbacks, and avoid any unescaped f-string tokens in automation heredocs.
5. **GCP REST APIs:** When querying Vertex AI (Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform) registries, always design REST request targets safely around the `/datasets/{}` endpoint schemas to bypass strict client SDK version mismatches.

# User Prompt
## Context
We successfully resolved a terminal copy-paste syntax error in `agents/read_saved_prompt.py` caused by unescaped `{}` curly braces inside raw heredocs. We have verified that the underlying prompt data in Agent Studio is stored structurally as a Vertex AI Dataset. 

Our target prompt dataset is ID: `2114685765899255808` under project `ctoteam` in region `us-central1`.

## Objective
Analyze our automated workspace configuration and implement the following next steps:
1. Ensure `configs/saved_prompt_config.json` is perfectly synced to use `prompt_id`: "2114685765899255808".
2. Verify that `agents/read_saved_prompt.py` is safely executed within the python virtual environment (`(venv)`) and successfully de-serializes the prompt's structural schema into `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md`.
3. Provide system testing feedback indicating if the final assembled markdown prompt was written successfully without any parsing omissions.

Yes, we can test from VM. Do this:

cd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run
mkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs

Create config:

cat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'
{
  "project_id": "ctoteam",
  "location": "us-central1",
  "prompt_id": "3364082818773680128",
  "model": "gemini-3.5-flash"
}
EOF

Create reader script:

cat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'
import json, subprocess, requests
from pathlib import Path

cfg = json.load(open("configs/saved_prompt_config.json"))
project = cfg["project_id"]
location = cfg["location"]
prompt_id = cfg["prompt_id"]

token = subprocess.check_output(
    ["gcloud", "auth", "print-access-token"], text=True
).strip()

headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
    "x-goog-user-project": project,
    "Content-Type": "application/json",
}

candidate_urls = [
    f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}",
    f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}",
    f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}",
    f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}",
]

Path("saved_prompts").mkdir(exist_ok=True)

for url in candidate_urls:
    print(f"\nTRY: {url}")
    r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
    print("STATUS:", r.status_code)

    if r.status_code == 200:
        data = r.json()
        out = f"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json"
        Path(out).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
        print(f"SUCCESS saved to {out}")
        print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:3000])
        break
    else:
        print(r.text[:1000])
else:
    print("\nFAILED: No API endpoint worked. Need SDK/API name verification.")
EOF

Run:

python3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q
python3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py

Also verify auth:

gcloud config set project ctoteam
gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam
gcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam

If this works, next step is:

saved_prompts/raw_3364082818773680128.json

Then we wire it into:

./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md

Key point: we read by prompt_id 3364082818773680128, not by the Console URL.

My focus is now on detailing these critical setup requirements.\\n\\n\",\"thought\":true},{\"text\":\"The issue you ran into is due to two distinct system/authentication limits:\\n1. **PEP 668 Restriction (`externally-managed-environment`):** Modern Python installations (like Python 3.12 on your VM) restrict installing global packages using `pip` without virtual environments.\\n2. **Expired `gcloud` Credentials:** The traceback from your python script occurred because `gcloud auth print-access-token` exited with status 1 due to expired authentication.\\n\\n### The \\\"Under-the-Hood\\\" Solution\\nIn Google Cloud, Vertex AI Studio prompt management is structurally backed by the **Vertex AI Dataset API** under the hood. When you save a prompt in Vertex AI Studio, Google creates a standard Dataset resource located at:\\n`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}`\\n\\nBy querying this REST API directly, you get direct access to the raw JSON of your saved prompt. This is extremely robust and **bypasses any strict Python SDK protobuf parser version conflicts** (which often crash on older Python client setups due to newer features in saved prompt models).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step-by-Step Recovery Guide\\n\\n#### Step 1: Fix `gcloud` Authentication\\nTo clear the expired auth on your remote VM, run the headless login command:\\n```bash\\ngcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\n```\\nCopy and paste the URL into your browser, grant permissions, and paste the verification code back into your terminal.\\n\\nNext, set up the project and quota project targets:\\n```bash\\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project\\u003dctoteam\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 2: Install Python Libraries Safely\\nYou can bypass the PEP 668 environment lock by adding the `--break-system-packages` flag, or by creating a clean virtual environment:\\n\\n**Option A (Quick command bypass):**\\n```bash\\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q --break-system-packages\\n```\\n\\n**Option B (Clean virtual environment - Recommended):**\\n```bash\\npython3 -m venv venv\\nsource venv/bin/activate\\npip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 3: Write the Config\\nCreate the workspace directories (if you haven\\u0027t yet) and write the configurations:\\n```bash\\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\\n```\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e configs/saved_prompt_config.json \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\n{\\n  \\\"project_id\\\": \\\"ctoteam\\\",\\n  \\\"location\\\": \\\"us-central1\\\",\\n  \\\"prompt_id\\\": \\\"3364082818773680128\\\",\\n  \\\"model\\\": \\\"gemini-3.5-flash\\\"\\n}\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 4: Write the Robust Dataset-based Reader Script\\nThis script targets the standard underlying Vertex AI Dataset API endpoint, parses the prompt\\u0027s structural schema, and outputs `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` in Markdown format:\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load config\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file not found. Ensure configs/saved_prompt_config.json exists.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: Failed to retrieve access token from gcloud.\\\")\\n    print(\\\"Please run the following in your terminal first to authenticate:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud config set project ctoteam\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"Bearer {token}\\\",\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# Vertex AI Studio Saved Prompts are structurally managed as Dataset resources\\nurl \\u003d f\\\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}\\\"\\n\\nprint(f\\\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST endpoint:\\\\n{url}\\\\n\\\")\\nr \\u003d requests.get(url, headers\\u003dheaders, timeout\\u003d30)\\n\\nprint(\\\"Status Code:\\\", r.status_code)\\n\\nif r.status_code !\\u003d 200:\\n    print(\\\"Error details:\\\")\\n    print(r.text)\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nIf you see a permission error, ensure you ran:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\ndata \\u003d r.json()\\n\\n# Ensure destination exists\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts\\\").mkdir(exist_ok\\u003dTrue)\\n\\n# Save the raw backend representation JSON\\nPath(f\\\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\\\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2))\\nprint(f\\\"Raw prompt metadata saved to: saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\\\")\\n\\n# Safely extract System Instructions and Prompt Text out of the structural schema\\nmetadata \\u003d data.get(\\\"metadata\\\", {})\\nprompt_api_schema \\u003d metadata.get(\\\"prompt_api_schema\\\", {})\\nprompt_message \\u003d prompt_api_schema.get(\\\"prompt_message\\\", {})\\n\\n# 1. Extract System Instructions\\nsystem_instruction_parts \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"systemInstruction\\\", {}).get(\\\"parts\\\", [])\\nsystem_instruction_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join([part.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\") for part in system_instruction_parts if \\\"text\\\" in part])\\n\\n# 2. Extract User Prompt Text\\ncontents \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"contents\\\", [])\\nuser_prompt_parts \\u003d []\\nfor content in contents:\\n    if content.get(\\\"role\\\") \\u003d\\u003d \\\"user\\\" or not content.get(\\\"role\\\"):\\n        for part in content.get(\\\"parts\\\", []):\\n            if \\\"text\\\" in part:\\n                user_prompt_parts.append(part[\\\"text\\\"])\\nuser_prompt_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(user_prompt_parts)\\n\\n# Construct the assembled Markdown file\\nassembled_md_content \\u003d f\\\"\\\"\\\"# System Instructions\\n{system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \\\"(None provided)\\\"}\\n\\n# User Prompt\\n{user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \\\"(None provided)\\\"}\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\\nprint(\\\"Assembled prompt written successfully to: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\n\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1500])\\nif len(assembled_md_content) \\u003e 1500:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n... [truncated for preview]\\\")\\nprint(\\\"----------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 5: Run and Verify\\nExecute your reader:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\\n\\nOnce appadmin@chn-mit-genai-dq1:~/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run$ there ius wayt that we cvan read in vm the promot anfd libve\nThought for 21s\nYes, we can test from VM. Do this:\n\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\n\nCreate config:\n\ncat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'\n{\n  \"project_id\": \"ctoteam\",\n  \"location\": \"us-central1\",\n  \"prompt_id\": \"3364082818773680128\",\n  \"model\": \"gemini-3.5-flash\"\n}\nEOF\n\nCreate reader script:\n\ncat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'\nimport json, subprocess, requests\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ncfg = json.load(open(\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\"))\nproject = cfg[\"project_id\"]\nlocation = cfg[\"location\"]\nprompt_id = cfg[\"prompt_id\"]\n\ntoken = subprocess.check_output(\n    [\"gcloud\", \"auth\", \"print-access-token\"], text=True\n).strip()\n\nheaders = {\n    \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {token}\",\n    \"x-goog-user-project\": project,\n    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n}\n\ncandidate_urls = [\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}\",\n]\n\nPath(\"saved_prompts\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\nfor url in candidate_urls:\n    print(f\"\\nTRY: {url}\")\n    r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)\n    print(\"STATUS:\", r.status_code)\n\n    if r.status_code == 200:\n        data = r.json()\n        out = f\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\"\n        Path(out).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\n        print(f\"SUCCESS saved to {out}\")\n        print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:3000])\n        break\n    else:\n        print(r.text[:1000])\nelse:\n    print(\"\\nFAILED: No API endpoint worked. Need SDK/API name verification.\")\nEOF\n\nRun:\n\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\n\nAlso verify auth:\n\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam\n\nIf this works, next step is:\n\nsaved_prompts/raw_3364082818773680128.json\n\nThen we wire it into:\n\n./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\n\nKey point: we read by prompt_id 3364082818773680128, not by the Console URL.\n"

This approach bypasses the earlier protobuf parsing issues and allows me to obtain the raw JSON data for further processing.\n\n**Extracting Prompt Details**\n\nI'm now successfully extracting system instructions and user prompt text directly from the retrieved JSON, reconstructing the prompt's content for `aider`. This bypasses previous parsing challenges and moves me closer to functional prompt assembly.\n\n**Confirming Vertex AI Setup**\n\nI've confirmed that enabling the `aiplatform.googleapis.com` service and setting the quota project are essential steps. This ensures smooth interaction with Vertex AI and prevents quota-related issues. My focus is now on detailing these critical setup requirements.\n\n",
                  "thought": true

                {
                  "text": "The issue you ran into is due to two distinct system/authentication limits:\n1. **PEP 668 Restriction (`externally-managed-environment`):** Modern Python installations (like Python 3.12 on your VM) restrict installing global packages using `pip` without virtual environments.\n2. **Expired `gcloud` Credentials:** The traceback from your python script occurred because `gcloud auth print-access-token` exited with status 1 due to expired authentication.\n\n### The \"Under-the-Hood\" Solution\nIn Google Cloud, Vertex AI Studio prompt management is structurally backed by the **Vertex AI Dataset API** under the hood. When you save a prompt in Vertex AI Studio, Google creates a standard Dataset resource located at:\n`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}`\n\nBy querying this REST API directly, you get direct access to the raw JSON of your saved prompt. This is extremely robust and **bypasses any strict Python SDK protobuf parser version conflicts** (which often crash on older Python client setups due to newer features in saved prompt models).\n\n---\n\n### Step-by-Step Recovery Guide\n\n#### Step 1: Fix `gcloud` Authentication\nTo clear the expired auth on your remote VM, run the headless login command:\n```bash\ngcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\n```\nCopy and paste the URL into your browser, grant permissions, and paste the verification code back into your terminal.\n\nNext, set up the project and quota project targets:\n```bash\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Install Python Libraries Safely\nYou can bypass the PEP 668 environment lock by adding the `--break-system-packages` flag, or by creating a clean virtual environment:\n\n**Option A (Quick command bypass):**\n```bash\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q --break-system-packages\n```\n\n**Option B (Clean virtual environment - Recommended):**\n```bash\npython3 -m venv venv\nsource venv/bin/activate\npip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Write the Config\nCreate the workspace directories (if you haven't yet) and write the configurations:\n```bash\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\n```\n\n```bash\ncat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'\n{\n  \"project_id\": \"ctoteam\",\n  \"location\": \"us-central1\",\n  \"prompt_id\": \"3364082818773680128\",\n  \"model\": \"gemini-3.5-flash\"\n}\nEOF\n```\n\n#### Step 4: Write the Robust Dataset-based Reader Script\nThis script targets the standard underlying Vertex AI Dataset API endpoint, parses the prompt's structural schema, and outputs `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` in Markdown format:\n\n```bash\ncat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport requests\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# Load config\ntry:\n    cfg = json.load(open(\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\"))\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    print(\"Config file not found. Ensure configs/saved_prompt_config.json exists.\")\n    exit(1)\n\nproject = cfg[\"project_id\"]\nlocation = cfg[\"location\"]\nprompt_id = cfg[\"prompt_id\"]\n\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\ntry:\n    token = subprocess.check_output(\n        [\"gcloud\", \"auth\", \"print-access-token\"], text=True\n    ).strip()\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n    print(\"\\nERROR: Failed to retrieve access token from gcloud.\")\n    print(\"Please run the following in your terminal first to authenticate:\")\n    print(\"  gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\")\n    print(\"  gcloud config set project ctoteam\")\n    exit(1)\n\nheaders = {\n    \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {token}\",\n    \"x-goog-user-project\": project,\n    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n}\n\n# Vertex AI Studio Saved Prompts are structurally managed as Dataset resources\nurl = f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}\"\n\nprint(f\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST endpoint:\\n{url}\\n\")\nr = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)\n\nprint(\"Status Code:\", r.status_code)\n\nif r.status_code != 200:\n    print(\"Error details:\")\n    print(r.text)\n    print(\"\\nIf you see a permission error, ensure you ran:\")\n    print(\"  gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\")\n    exit(1)\n\ndata = r.json()\n\n# Ensure destination exists\nPath(\"saved_prompts\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n# Save the raw backend representation JSON\nPath(f\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\nprint(f\"Raw prompt metadata saved to: saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\")\n\n# Safely extract System Instructions and Prompt Text out of the structural schema\nmetadata = data.get(\"metadata\", {})\nprompt_api_schema = metadata.get(\"prompt_api_schema\", {})\nprompt_message = prompt_api_schema.get(\"prompt_message\", {})\n\n# 1. Extract System Instructions\nsystem_instruction_parts = prompt_message.get(\"systemInstruction\", {}).get(\"parts\", [])\nsystem_instruction_text = \"\\n\".join([part.get(\"text\", \"\") for part in system_instruction_parts if \"text\" in part])\n\n# 2. Extract User Prompt Text\ncontents = prompt_message.get(\"contents\", [])\nuser_prompt_parts = []\nfor content in contents:\n    if content.get(\"role\") == \"user\" or not content.get(\"role\"):\n        for part in content.get(\"parts\", []):\n            if \"text\" in part:\n                user_prompt_parts.append(part[\"text\"])\nuser_prompt_text = \"\\n\".join(user_prompt_parts)\n\n# Construct the assembled Markdown file\nassembled_md_content = f\"\"\"# System Instructions\n{system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \"(None provided)\"}\n\n# User Prompt\n{user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \"(None provided)\"}\n\"\"\"\n\nPath(\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\nprint(\"Assembled prompt written successfully to: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\")\n\nprint(\"\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\")\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1500])\nif len(assembled_md_content) > 1500:\n    print(\"\\n... [truncated for preview]\")\nprint(\"----------------------\")\nEOF\n```\n\n#### Step 5: Run and Verify\nExecute your reader:\n```bash\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\n```\n\nOnce it returns a successful preview, launch aider using your newly read prompt:\n```bash\n./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\n```",

Thought for 21s
Yes, we can test from VM. Do this:

cd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run
mkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs

Create config:

cat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'
{
 "project_id": "ctoteam",
 "location": "us-central1",
 "prompt_id": "3364082818773680128",
 "model": "gemini-3.5-flash"
}
EOF

Create reader script:

cat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'
import json, subprocess, requests
from pathlib import Path

cfg = json.load(open("configs/saved_prompt_config.json"))
project = cfg["project_id"]
location = cfg["location"]
prompt_id = cfg["prompt_id"]

token = subprocess.check_output(
  ["gcloud", "auth", "print-access-token"], text=True
).strip()

headers = {
  "Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
  "x-goog-user-project": project,
  "Content-Type": "application/json",
}

candidate_urls = [
  f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}",
  f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}",
  f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}",
  f"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}",
]

Path("saved_prompts").mkdir(exist_ok=True)

for url in candidate_urls:
  print(f"\nTRY: {url}")
  r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)
  print("STATUS:", r.status_code)

  if r.status_code == 200:
    data = r.json()
    out = f"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json"
    Path(out).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))
    print(f"SUCCESS saved to {out}")
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:3000])
    break
  else:
    print(r.text[:1000])
else:
  print("\nFAILED: No API endpoint worked. Need SDK/API name verification.")
EOF

Run:

python3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q
python3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py

Also verify auth:

gcloud config set project ctoteam
gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam
gcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam

If this works, next step is:

saved_prompts/raw_3364082818773680128.json

Then we wire it into:

./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md

Key point: we read by prompt_id 3364082818773680128, not by the Console URL.

My focus is now on detailing these critical setup requirements.\\n\\n\",\"thought\":true},{\"text\":\"The issue you ran into is due to two distinct system/authentication limits:\\n1. **PEP 668 Restriction (`externally-managed-environment`):** Modern Python installations (like Python 3.12 on your VM) restrict installing global packages using `pip` without virtual environments.\\n2. **Expired `gcloud` Credentials:** The traceback from your python script occurred because `gcloud auth print-access-token` exited with status 1 due to expired authentication.\\n\\n### The \\\"Under-the-Hood\\\" Solution\\nIn Google Cloud, Vertex AI Studio prompt management is structurally backed by the **Vertex AI Dataset API** under the hood. When you save a prompt in Vertex AI Studio, Google creates a standard Dataset resource located at:\\n`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}`\\n\\nBy querying this REST API directly, you get direct access to the raw JSON of your saved prompt. This is extremely robust and **bypasses any strict Python SDK protobuf parser version conflicts** (which often crash on older Python client setups due to newer features in saved prompt models).\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step-by-Step Recovery Guide\\n\\n#### Step 1: Fix `gcloud` Authentication\\nTo clear the expired auth on your remote VM, run the headless login command:\\n```bash\\ngcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\n```\\nCopy and paste the URL into your browser, grant permissions, and paste the verification code back into your terminal.\\n\\nNext, set up the project and quota project targets:\\n```bash\\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project\\u003dctoteam\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 2: Install Python Libraries Safely\\nYou can bypass the PEP 668 environment lock by adding the `--break-system-packages` flag, or by creating a clean virtual environment:\\n\\n**Option A (Quick command bypass):**\\n```bash\\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q --break-system-packages\\n```\\n\\n**Option B (Clean virtual environment - Recommended):**\\n```bash\\npython3 -m venv venv\\nsource venv/bin/activate\\npip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 3: Write the Config\\nCreate the workspace directories (if you haven\\u0027t yet) and write the configurations:\\n```bash\\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\\n```\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e configs/saved_prompt_config.json \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\n{\\n  \\\"project_id\\\": \\\"ctoteam\\\",\\n  \\\"location\\\": \\\"us-central1\\\",\\n  \\\"prompt_id\\\": \\\"3364082818773680128\\\",\\n  \\\"model\\\": \\\"gemini-3.5-flash\\\"\\n}\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 4: Write the Robust Dataset-based Reader Script\\nThis script targets the standard underlying Vertex AI Dataset API endpoint, parses the prompt\\u0027s structural schema, and outputs `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` in Markdown format:\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load config\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file not found. Ensure configs/saved_prompt_config.json exists.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: Failed to retrieve access token from gcloud.\\\")\\n    print(\\\"Please run the following in your terminal first to authenticate:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud config set project ctoteam\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": f\\\"Bearer {token}\\\",\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# Vertex AI Studio Saved Prompts are structurally managed as Dataset resources\\nurl \\u003d f\\\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}\\\"\\n\\nprint(f\\\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST endpoint:\\\\n{url}\\\\n\\\")\\nr \\u003d requests.get(url, headers\\u003dheaders, timeout\\u003d30)\\n\\nprint(\\\"Status Code:\\\", r.status_code)\\n\\nif r.status_code !\\u003d 200:\\n    print(\\\"Error details:\\\")\\n    print(r.text)\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nIf you see a permission error, ensure you ran:\\\")\\n    print(\\\"  gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\ndata \\u003d r.json()\\n\\n# Ensure destination exists\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts\\\").mkdir(exist_ok\\u003dTrue)\\n\\n# Save the raw backend representation JSON\\nPath(f\\\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\\\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2))\\nprint(f\\\"Raw prompt metadata saved to: saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\\\")\\n\\n# Safely extract System Instructions and Prompt Text out of the structural schema\\nmetadata \\u003d data.get(\\\"metadata\\\", {})\\nprompt_api_schema \\u003d metadata.get(\\\"prompt_api_schema\\\", {})\\nprompt_message \\u003d prompt_api_schema.get(\\\"prompt_message\\\", {})\\n\\n# 1. Extract System Instructions\\nsystem_instruction_parts \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"systemInstruction\\\", {}).get(\\\"parts\\\", [])\\nsystem_instruction_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join([part.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\") for part in system_instruction_parts if \\\"text\\\" in part])\\n\\n# 2. Extract User Prompt Text\\ncontents \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"contents\\\", [])\\nuser_prompt_parts \\u003d []\\nfor content in contents:\\n    if content.get(\\\"role\\\") \\u003d\\u003d \\\"user\\\" or not content.get(\\\"role\\\"):\\n        for part in content.get(\\\"parts\\\", []):\\n            if \\\"text\\\" in part:\\n                user_prompt_parts.append(part[\\\"text\\\"])\\nuser_prompt_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(user_prompt_parts)\\n\\n# Construct the assembled Markdown file\\nassembled_md_content \\u003d f\\\"\\\"\\\"# System Instructions\\n{system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \\\"(None provided)\\\"}\\n\\n# User Prompt\\n{user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \\\"(None provided)\\\"}\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\\nprint(\\\"Assembled prompt written successfully to: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\n\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1500])\\nif len(assembled_md_content) \\u003e 1500:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\n... [truncated for preview]\\\")\\nprint(\\\"----------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n#### Step 5: Run and Verify\\nExecute your reader:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\\n\\nOnce This explains the parsing complexities encountered.\\n\\n**Resolving Schema Ambiguity**\\n\\nI\\u0027ve identified two distinct schema paths for prompt data: one within `data[\\\"metadata\\\"][\\\"text\\\"]` (camelCase) and another in `data[\\\"promptApiSchema\\\"]` (snake_case). My current focus is on implementing a unified parsing strategy to handle both gracefully.\\n\\n**Unifying Schema Handling**\\n\\nI\\u0027ve pinpointed two schema structures: one is a camelCase JSON string within `data[\\\"metadata\\\"][\\\"text\\\"]`, the other is snake_case in `data[\\\"promptApiSchema\\\"]`. My current objective is to develop a unified parsing approach to manage both these data formats effectively.\\n\\n**Synthesizing Prompt Data**\\n\\nI\\u0027m now combining the system instructions with the user prompt parts I\\u0027ve extracted. My current objective is to ensure this integrated prompt is correctly formatted before further processing.\\n\\n**Combining Prompt Segments**\\n\\nI\\u0027m now merging the system instructions with the user prompt components I\\u0027ve gathered. My focus is on ensuring the final, unified prompt is accurately structured for the next stages of processing.\\n\\n**Assembling Final Prompt**\\n\\nI\\u0027m now merging extracted system instructions with user prompt segments. My current goal is to ensure the fully integrated prompt is accurately structured for subsequent processing, preparing the markdown file.\\n\\n\",\"thought\":true},{\"text\":\"Excellent find! Seeing the raw JSON output is incredibly helpful. \\n\\n### Why the previous parser didn\\u0027t catch the prompt text\\nAs your JSON dump reveals, the Agent Platform / Vertex AI API structures prompt data in two parallel ways:\\n1. **Pathway 1 (Serialized JSON String):** The `metadata` object contains a field named `\\\"text\\\"`, which is a **serialized JSON string** containing the full `structured_prompt` structure.\\n2. **Pathway 2 (Nested Dictionary):** The API exposes a camelCase schema under `promptApiSchema` -\\u003e `multimodalPrompt` -\\u003e `promptMessage`.\\n\\nBecause the core data inside `\\\"metadata\\\"` was wrapped in an extra layer of string-serialization (i.e. as a raw string), the previous parser skipped right over it.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 1: Overwrite with the Robust Double-Parser\\nThis updated script dynamically tries **both pathways** (de-serializing the `metadata.text` JSON string, and falling back to the nested `promptApiSchema` structure) to guarantee flawless extraction of your prompt details.\\n\\nRun this command in your VM terminal:\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load configuration\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file configs/saved_prompt_config.json not found.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed.\\\")\\n    print(\\\"Please run: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": \\\"Bearer \\\" + token,\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# The Vertex AI / Agent Platform dataset endpoint\\nurl \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/datasets/\\\" + prompt_id\\n\\nprint(\\\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST API...\\\")\\nr \\u003d requests.get(url, headers\\u003dheaders, timeout\\u003d30)\\nprint(\\\"Status Code:\\\", r.status_code)\\n\\nif r.status_code !\\u003d 200:\\n    print(\\\"Error details:\\\", r.text)\\n    exit(1)\\n\\ndata \\u003d r.json()\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts\\\").mkdir(exist_ok\\u003dTrue)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2))\\nprint(\\\"Saved raw metadata to saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\")\\n\\n# Parsing variables\\nsystem_instruction_text \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\nuser_prompt_text \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\n\\n# Pathway 1: Parse the JSON-serialized \\\"text\\\" string inside \\\"metadata\\\"\\nmetadata \\u003d data.get(\\\"metadata\\\", {})\\ntext_str \\u003d metadata.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n\\nif text_str:\\n    try:\\n        text_json \\u003d json.loads(text_str)\\n        structured_prompt \\u003d text_json.get(\\\"structured_prompt\\\", {})\\n        prompt_message \\u003d structured_prompt.get(\\\"prompt_message\\\", {})\\n        \\n        # Extract System Instructions (supports both snake_case and camelCase inside serialized text)\\n        sys_inst \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"system_instruction\\\", {})\\n        if not sys_inst:\\n            sys_inst \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"systemInstruction\\\", {})\\n        sys_parts \\u003d sys_inst.get(\\\"parts\\\", [])\\n        system_instruction_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join([p.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\") for p in sys_parts if \\\"text\\\" in p])\\n        \\n        # Extract User contents\\n        contents \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"contents\\\", [])\\n        user_prompt_parts \\u003d []\\n        for content in contents:\\n            if content.get(\\\"role\\\") \\u003d\\u003d \\\"user\\\" or not content.get(\\\"role\\\"):\\n                for p in content.get(\\\"parts\\\", []):\\n                    if \\\"text\\\" in p:\\n                        user_prompt_parts.append(p[\\\"text\\\"])\\n        user_prompt_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(user_prompt_parts)\\n        print(\\\"Successfully extracted prompt content from metadata.text JSON string.\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(\\\"Warning: Failed to parse metadata.text as JSON string:\\\", e)\\n\\n# Pathway 2 (Fallback): Parse promptApiSchema\\nif not user_prompt_text and not system_instruction_text:\\n    prompt_api_schema \\u003d data.get(\\\"promptApiSchema\\\", {})\\n    \\n    # Handle standard nested pathways\\n    prompt_message \\u003d prompt_api_schema.get(\\\"multimodalPrompt\\\", {}).get(\\\"promptMessage\\\", {})\\n    if not prompt_message:\\n        prompt_message \\u003d prompt_api_schema.get(\\\"promptMessage\\\", {})\\n        \\n    if prompt_message:\\n        sys_parts \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"systemInstruction\\\", {}).get(\\\"parts\\\", [])\\n        system_instruction_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join([p.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\") for p in sys_parts if \\\"text\\\" in p])\\n        \\n        contents \\u003d prompt_message.get(\\\"contents\\\", [])\\n        user_prompt_parts \\u003d []\\n        for content in contents:\\n            if content.get(\\\"role\\\") \\u003d\\u003d \\\"user\\\" or not content.get(\\\"role\\\"):\\n                for p in content.get(\\\"parts\\\", []):\\n                    if \\\"text\\\" in p:\\n                        user_prompt_parts.append(p[\\\"text\\\"])\\n        user_prompt_text \\u003d \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(user_prompt_parts)\\n        print(\\\"Successfully extracted prompt content via promptApiSchema fallback.\\\")\\n\\n# Assemble Markdown file\\nassembled_md_content \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\\"# System Instructions\\n\\\"\\\"\\\" + (system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \\\"(None)\\\") + \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n# User Prompt\\n\\\"\\\"\\\" + (user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \\\"(None)\\\") + \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\\nprint(\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1500])\\nprint(\\\"----------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Run the Script\\nExecute the updated python agent:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\\n\\nThis will cleanly de-serialize the prompt, generate `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` Gemini acts as an expert Prompt Engineer, reading the raw JSON structure, extracting the relevant chat history, stripping out raw escape characters, and formatting it into a beautiful, production-ready markdown file.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 1: Ensure Config is perfectly Synced\\nRun this to configure the dataset ID `2114685765899255808`:\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e configs/saved_prompt_config.json \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\n{\\n  \\\"project_id\\\": \\\"ctoteam\\\",\\n  \\\"location\\\": \\\"us-central1\\\",\\n  \\\"prompt_id\\\": \\\"2114685765899255808\\\",\\n  \\\"model\\\": \\\"gemini-3.5-flash\\\"\\n}\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Overwrite with the Dynamic Gemini-Powered Reader Script\\nRun this command on your terminal to deploy the dynamically parsing reader script:\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load configuration\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file configs/saved_prompt_config.json not found.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed.\\\")\\n    print(\\\"Please run: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": \\\"Bearer \\\" + token,\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# 1. Fetch saved prompt dataset from REST API\\nurl \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/datasets/\\\" + prompt_id\\n\\nprint(\\\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST API...\\\")\\nr \\u003d requests.get(url, headers\\u003dheaders, timeout\\u003d30)\\nprint(\\\"Dataset Fetch Status Code:\\\", r.status_code)\\n\\nif r.status_code !\\u003d 200:\\n    print(\\\"Error details:\\\", r.text)\\n    exit(1)\\n\\ndata \\u003d r.json()\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts\\\").mkdir(exist_ok\\u003dTrue)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2))\\nprint(\\\"Saved raw metadata to saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\")\\n\\n# 2. Use Gemini 3.5 Flash to dynamically read, parse, and generate the aligned prompt\\nprint(\\\"\\\\nInvoking Gemini 3.5 Flash to dynamically parse the JSON dataset and generate an optimized prompt...\\\")\\n\\nsystem_instruction \\u003d (\\n    \\\"You are an expert prompt engineer. You are given a raw JSON representation of a saved prompt dataset \\\"\\n    \\\"from the Google Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (Vertex AI). Your task is to analyze this raw JSON, \\\"\\n    \\\"find the system instructions and user chat messages/prompts, clean them up, align them properly, and \\\"\\n    \\\"generate a highly optimized Markdown prompt designed for use by an agentic coding assistant like Aider.\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    \\\"The output must be a clean markdown file structured with `# System Instructions` and `# User Prompt` sections. \\\"\\n    \\\"Do not include any conversational preamble or markdown code fences like ```markdown. Output ONLY the raw markdown content.\\\"\\n)\\n\\nprompt_payload \\u003d {\\n    \\\"contents\\\": [\\n        {\\n            \\\"role\\\": \\\"user\\\",\\n            \\\"parts\\\": [\\n                {\\n                    \\\"text\\\": \\\"Here is the raw JSON representation of the saved prompt dataset:\\\\n\\\\n\\\" + json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2)\\n                }\\n            ]\\n        }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"systemInstruction\\\": {\\n        \\\"parts\\\": [\\n            {\\n                \\\"text\\\": system_instruction\\n            }\\n        ]\\n    },\\n    \\\"generationConfig\\\": {\\n        \\\"temperature\\\": 0.2,\\n        \\\"maxOutputTokens\\\": 8192\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n# Try gemini-3.5-flash first, fallback to gemini-1.5-flash if needed\\nmodels \\u003d [\\\"gemini-3.5-flash\\\", \\\"gemini-1.5-flash\\\"]\\ngeneration_success \\u003d False\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\n\\nfor model in models:\\n    gemini_url \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/publishers/google/models/\\\" + model + \\\":generateContent\\\"\\n    print(f\\\"Trying generation with model: {model}...\\\")\\n    \\n    gen_response \\u003d requests.post(gemini_url, headers\\u003dheaders, json\\u003dprompt_payload, timeout\\u003d60)\\n    print(\\\"Generation Status Code:\\\", gen_response.status_code)\\n    \\n    if gen_response.status_code \\u003d\\u003d 200:\\n        gen_data \\u003d gen_response.json()\\n        try:\\n            generated_prompt \\u003d gen_data[\\\"candidates\\\"][0][\\\"content\\\"][\\\"parts\\\"][0][\\\"text\\\"]\\n            generation_success \\u003d True\\n            print(f\\\"Success! Prompt generated successfully using {model}.\\\")\\n            break\\n        except (KeyError, IndexError) as e:\\n            print(f\\\"Failed to parse generation response for {model}: {e}\\\")\\n    else:\\n        print(f\\\"Model {model} failed with status {gen_response.status_code}: {gen_response.text[:1000]}\\\")\\n\\nif not generation_success:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: Failed to generate prompt with all tried models.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\n# Clean up any accidental ```markdown fences if the model output them despite instructions\\nif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```markdown\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[11:]\\nelif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[3:]\\nif generated_prompt.endswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[:-3]\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt.strip()\\n\\n# 3. Save the dynamically generated prompts\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\n\\nprint(f\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\nprint(f\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/{prompt_id}.md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- DYNAMIC PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(generated_prompt[:1500])\\nprint(\\\"------------------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Run the Script\\nNow run the script inside your active virtual environment:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\\n\\nThis will call the Vertex API, download the JSON representing your dataset, feed it back to Gemini 3.5 Flash, and cleanly output `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` and `saved_prompts/2114685765899255808.md`!\",\"thought_signature\":\"\"}]},{\"role\":\"user\",\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"EOFnt(\\\"------------------------------\\\")\\\")mpts/{}.md\\\")ed_prompt.md\\\")mpt)espite instructions.text[:10\\n(venv) appadmin@chn-mit-genai-dq1:~/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run$ python3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n\u00a0File \\\"/home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run/agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\\", line 100\\n\u00a0\u00a0generated_prompt \\u003d gen_data[\\\"candidates\\\"] 0 [\\\"content\\\"][\\\"parts\\\"] 0 [\\\"text\\\"]\\n\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0\u00a0^\\nSyntaxError: invalid syntax\\n(venv) appadmin@chn-mit-genai-dq1:~/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run$\\n\\n\"}]},{\"role\":\"model\",\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"**Fixing Syntax Errors**\\n\\nI\\u0027ve identified and corrected a syntax error in accessing nested dictionary values. The issue stemmed from incorrect indexing, which I\\u0027ve now resolved by using proper bracket notation for array access.\\n\\n**Investigating Data Retrieval**\\n\\nI\\u0027m currently investigating an unexpected issue where bracketed indices around numerical keys, specifically `[0]`, appear to be corrupted or stripped during terminal copy-pasting. This is leading to incorrect data access in my generated code. I\\u0027m exploring alternative methods for data retrieval to circumvent this behavior.\\n\\n**Eliminating Indexing Issues**\\n\\nI\\u0027ve devised an ingenious solution to bypass the bracket stripping problem entirely. By replacing direct list indexing like `[0]` with `next(iter(...))`, I can safely retrieve the first element of any list, even within nested structures. This approach ensures absolute immunity to the VM\\u0027s paste corruption, and I\\u0027m now systematically applying it throughout the codebase.\\n\\n**Adapting File Access**\\n\\nI\\u0027m shifting my strategy to directly writing to files within the designated project directory using Python, rather than relying on copy-pasting. This bypasses the observed issue with terminal emulators corrupting bracketed indices, ensuring data integrity and more robust automation.\\n\\n**Refining Element Access**\\n\\nI\\u0027m transitioning from `next(iter(...))` to a more elegant solution. By utilizing simple `for` loops to iterate through lists, I can access the first element without any bracketed indices, completely circumventing the terminal\\u0027s potential corruption issues. I am now systematically refactoring the codebase with this cleaner, more robust approach.\\n\\n\",\"thought\":true},{\"text\":\"The syntax error happened because your terminal emulator\\u0027s copy-paste handler stripped out the brackets around `[0]`, mangling it into ` 0 `. \\n\\nTo prevent any copy-paste or terminal-mangling issues on your SSH shell, here is an updated version of the script. This version **does not use any list indexing brackets like `[0]` or `[1]`**; instead, it uses clean, standard Python `for` loops to fetch the first items. This makes the code completely syntax-proof and immune to VM paste mangling!\\n\\n### Deploy the Syntax-Proofed Reader Script\\n\\nRun this command in your terminal:\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load configuration\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file configs/saved_prompt_config.json not found.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed.\\\")\\n    print(\\\"Please run: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": \\\"Bearer \\\" + token,\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# 1. Fetch saved prompt dataset from REST API\\nurl \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/datasets/\\\" + prompt_id\\n\\nprint(\\\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST API...\\\")\\nr \\u003d requests.get(url, headers\\u003dheaders, timeout\\u003d30)\\nprint(\\\"Dataset Fetch Status Code:\\\", r.status_code)\\n\\nif r.status_code !\\u003d 200:\\n    print(\\\"Error details:\\\", r.text)\\n    exit(1)\\n\\ndata \\u003d r.json()\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts\\\").mkdir(exist_ok\\u003dTrue)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2))\\nprint(\\\"Saved raw metadata to saved_prompts/raw_\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".json\\\")\\n\\n# 2. Use Gemini 3.5 Flash to dynamically read, parse, and generate the aligned prompt\\nprint(\\\"\\\\nInvoking Gemini 3.5 Flash to dynamically parse the JSON dataset and generate an optimized prompt...\\\")\\n\\nsystem_instruction \\u003d (\\n    \\\"You are an expert prompt engineer. You are given a raw JSON representation of a saved prompt dataset \\\"\\n    \\\"from the Google Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (Vertex AI). Your task is to analyze this raw JSON, \\\"\\n    \\\"find the system instructions and user chat messages/prompts, clean them up, align them properly, and \\\"\\n    \\\"generate a highly optimized Markdown prompt designed for use by an agentic coding assistant like Aider.\\\\n\\\\n\\\"\\n    \\\"The output must be a clean markdown file structured with `# System Instructions` and `# User Prompt` sections. \\\"\\n    \\\"Do not include any conversational preamble or markdown code fences like ```markdown. Output ONLY the raw markdown content.\\\"\\n)\\n\\nprompt_payload \\u003d {\\n    \\\"contents\\\": [\\n        {\\n            \\\"role\\\": \\\"user\\\",\\n            \\\"parts\\\": [\\n                {\\n                    \\\"text\\\": \\\"Here is the raw JSON representation of the saved prompt dataset:\\\\n\\\\n\\\" + json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2)\\n                }\\n            ]\\n        }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"systemInstruction\\\": {\\n        \\\"parts\\\": [\\n            {\\n                \\\"text\\\": system_instruction\\n            }\\n        ]\\n    },\\n    \\\"generationConfig\\\": {\\n        \\\"temperature\\\": 0.2,\\n        \\\"maxOutputTokens\\\": 8192\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n# Try gemini-3.5-flash first, fallback to gemini-1.5-flash if needed\\nmodels \\u003d [\\\"gemini-3.5-flash\\\", \\\"gemini-1.5-flash\\\"]\\ngeneration_success \\u003d False\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\n\\nfor model in models:\\n    gemini_url \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/publishers/google/models/\\\" + model + \\\":generateContent\\\"\\n    print(\\\"Trying generation with model:\\\", model)\\n    \\n    gen_response \\u003d requests.post(gemini_url, headers\\u003dheaders, json\\u003dprompt_payload, timeout\\u003d60)\\n    print(\\\"Generation Status Code:\\\", gen_response.status_code)\\n    \\n    if gen_response.status_code \\u003d\\u003d 200:\\n        gen_data \\u003d gen_response.json()\\n        \\n        # Safely extract generated prompt using loops instead of index brackets\\n        candidates \\u003d gen_data.get(\\\"candidates\\\", [])\\n        for candidate in candidates:\\n            parts \\u003d candidate.get(\\\"content\\\", {}).get(\\\"parts\\\", [])\\n            for part in parts:\\n                text_content \\u003d part.get(\\\"text\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n                if text_content:\\n                    generated_prompt \\u003d text_content\\n                    generation_success \\u003d True\\n                    break\\n            if generation_success:\\n                break\\n        \\n        if generation_success:\\n            print(\\\"Success! Prompt generated successfully using\\\", model)\\n            break\\n    else:\\n        print(\\\"Model\\\", model, \\\"failed with status\\\", gen_response.status_code)\\n\\nif not generation_success:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: Failed to generate prompt with all tried models.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\n# Clean up any accidental ```markdown fences if the model output them despite instructions\\nif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```markdown\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[11:]\\nelif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[3:]\\nif generated_prompt.endswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[:-3]\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt.strip()\\n\\n# 3. Save the dynamically generated prompts\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\n\\nprint(\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- DYNAMIC PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(generated_prompt[:1500])\\nprint(\\\"------------------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Run and Verify\\nExecute the script to trigger Gemini 3.5 Flash:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\"}]},{\"role\":\"user\",\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n\u00a0\u00a0print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed.\\\")\\n\u00a0\u00a0print(\\\"Please run: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\\\")\\nEOFnt(\\\"------------------------------\\\")\\\")pts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\")mpt)espite instructions\\u003d60) \\\"/lo\\n(venv) appadmin@chn-mit-genai-dq1:~/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run$ python3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\nFetching saved prompt dataset from REST API...\\nDataset Fetch Status Code: 200\\nSaved raw metadata to saved_prompts/raw_2114685765899255808.json\\n\\nInvoking Gemini 3.5 Flash to dynamically parse the JSON dataset and generate an optimized prompt...\\nTrying generation with model: gemini-3.5-flash\\nGeneration Status Code: 404\\nModel gemini-3.5-flash failed with status 404\\nTrying generation with model: gemini-1.5-flash\\nGeneration Status Code: 404\\nModel gemini-1.5-flash failed with status 404\\n\\nERROR: Failed to generate prompt with all tried models.\\n(venv) appadmin@chn-mit-genai-dq1:~/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run$\\n\\n we have adc we ned cto alksio 12.5 is obselte use 2.5 flash\"}]},{\"role\":\"model\",\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"**Processing User Input**\\n\\nI\\u0027m currently analyzing the user\\u0027s cryptic input. I\\u0027m working to decipher the acronyms and the intended action.\\n\\n**Updating Model References**\\n\\nMy thoughts confirm that \\\"12.5\\\" was a typo and should be \\\"1.5,\\\" indicating that Gemini 1.5 Flash is obsolete. I\\u0027ve identified `gemini-2.5-flash` as the correct, currently available stable model on Vertex AI and plan to update the models array and configuration file accordingly.\\n\\n**Investigating Model Availability**\\n\\nI\\u0027ve discovered that `gemini-3.5-flash` returned a 404 error because it\\u0027s not yet generally available or configured for the specific endpoint. If retrieving an access token via `gcloud auth print-access-token` fails, the script will now attempt `gcloud auth application-default print-access-token` to ensure token availability.\\n\\n\",\"thought\":true},{\"text\":\"Using **Gemini 2.5 Flash** (`gemini-2.5-flash`) via your VM\\u0027s Application Default Credentials (ADC) is the perfect solution.\\n\\nTo handle ADC and avoid any copy-paste ssh truncation, this script:\\n1. **Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash** (`gemini-2.5-flash`) as the primary generation engine.\\n2. **First queries standard user credentials, then automatically falls back to Application Default Credentials (ADC)** (`gcloud auth application-default print-access-token`) to obtain a token cleanly.\\n3. **Avoids any bracket list indexers** (`[0]`) to ensure 100% syntax-safety on SSH terminal pasting.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 1: Update Prompt Config to Gemini 2.5 Flash\\nOverwrite the config to use `\\\"gemini-2.5-flash\\\"`:\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e configs/saved_prompt_config.json \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\n{\\n  \\\"project_id\\\": \\\"ctoteam\\\",\\n  \\\"location\\\": \\\"us-central1\\\",\\n  \\\"prompt_id\\\": \\\"2114685765899255808\\\",\\n  \\\"model\\\": \\\"gemini-2.5-flash\\\"\\n}\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 2: Deploy the ADC-Compatible, Gemini 2.5 Flash Reader Script\\nRun this to update your Python agent script:\\n\\n```bash\\ncat \\u003e agents/read_saved_prompt.py \\u003c\\u003c\\u0027EOF\\u0027\\nimport json\\nimport subprocess\\nimport requests\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\n# Load configuration\\ntry:\\n    cfg \\u003d json.load(open(\\\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\\\"))\\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\\n    print(\\\"Config file configs/saved_prompt_config.json not found.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\nproject \\u003d cfg[\\\"project_id\\\"]\\nlocation \\u003d cfg[\\\"location\\\"]\\nprompt_id \\u003d cfg[\\\"prompt_id\\\"]\\n\\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud, falling back to ADC if needed\\ntoken \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\ntry:\\n    token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n        [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n    ).strip()\\n    print(\\\"Successfully retrieved user access token.\\\")\\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n    try:\\n        print(\\\"User access token retrieval failed. Trying Application Default Credentials (ADC)...\\\")\\n        token \\u003d subprocess.check_output(\\n            [\\\"gcloud\\\", \\\"auth\\\", \\\"application-default\\\", \\\"print-access-token\\\"], text\\u003dTrue\\n        ).strip()\\n        print(\\\"Successfully retrieved ADC access token.\\\")\\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\\n        print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed for both user and ADC profiles.\\\")\\n        print(\\\"Please run: gcloud auth application-default login\\\")\\n        exit(1)\\n\\nheaders \\u003d {\\n    \\\"Authorization\\\": \\\"Bearer \\\" + token,\\n    \\\"x-goog-user-project\\\": project,\\n    \\\"Content-Type\\\": \\\"application/json\\\",\\n}\\n\\n# 1. Use Gemini 2.5 Flash to dynamically read, parse, and generate the aligned prompt\\nprint(\\\"\\\\nInvoking Gemini 2.5 Flash to dynamically parse the JSON dataset and generate an optimized prompt...\\\")\\n\\nsystem_instruction \\u003d (\\n    \\\"You are an expert prompt engineer. Output ONLY the raw markdown content.\\\"\\n)\\n\\nprompt_payload \\u003d {\\n    \\\"contents\\\": [\\n        {\\n            \\\"role\\\": \\\"user\\\",\\n            \\\"parts\\\": [\\n                {\\n                    \\\"text\\\": \\\"Here is the raw JSON representation of the saved prompt dataset:\\\\n\\\\n\\\" + json.dumps(data, indent\\u003d2)\\n                }\\n            ]\\n        }\\n    ],\\n    \\\"systemInstruction\\\": {\\n        \\\"parts\\\": [\\n            {\\n                \\\"text\\\": system_instruction\\n            }\\n        ]\\n    },\\n    \\\"generationConfig\\\": {\\n        \\\"temperature\\\": 0.2,\\n        \\\"maxOutputTokens\\\": 8192\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n# Try gemini-2.5-flash first, fallback to gemini-2.5-flash-lite if needed\\nmodels \\u003d [\\\"gemini-2.5-flash\\\", \\\"gemini-2.5-flash-lite\\\"]\\ngeneration_success \\u003d False\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d \\\"\\\"\\n\\nfor model in models:\\n    gemini_url \\u003d \\\"https://\\\" + location + \\\"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\\\" + project + \\\"/locations/\\\" + location + \\\"/publishers/google/models/\\\" + model + \\\":generateContent\\\"\\n    print(\\\"Trying generation with model:\\\", model)\\n    \\n    gen_response \\u003d requests.post(gemini_url, headers\\u003dheaders, json\\u003dprompt_payload, timeout\\u003d60)\\n    print(\\\"Generation Status Code:\\\", gen_response.status_code)\\n    \\n    if gen_response.status_code \\u003d\\u003d 200:\\n        gen_data \\u003d gen_response.json()\\n        \\n        # Prompt generated successfully using\\\", model)\\n            break\\n    else:\\n        print(\\\"Model\\\", model, \\\"failed with status\\\", gen_response.status_code)\\n\\nif not generation_success:\\n    print(\\\"\\\\nERROR: Failed to generate prompt with all tried models.\\\")\\n    exit(1)\\n\\n# Clean up any accidental ```markdown fences if the model output them despite instructions\\nif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```markdown\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[11:]\\nelif generated_prompt.startswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[3:]\\nif generated_prompt.endswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n    generated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt[:-3]\\ngenerated_prompt \\u003d generated_prompt.strip()\\n\\n# 3. Save the dynamically generated prompts\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\nPath(\\\"saved_prompts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\").write_text(generated_prompt)\\n\\nprint(\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/\\\" + prompt_id + \\\".md\\\")\\nprint(\\\"\\\\n--- DYNAMIC PROMPT PREVIEW ---\\\")\\nprint(generated_prompt[:1500])\\nprint(\\\"------------------------------\\\")\\nEOF\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Step 3: Run the Script\\nNow run the script inside your active virtual environment:\\n```bash\\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\\n```\",\"thought_signature\":\"\"}]}],\"system_instruction\":{\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"# System Instructions\\nYou are an elite, principal software engineering agent powered by Gemini 3.5 Flash.\\nYour execution workspace is `/home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run/`.\\n\\nYour behavior rules and coding standards:\\n1. **Scope:** Your focus is to build, patch, and execute robust python automation utilities, configuration files, and API helper scripts under this directory.\\n2. **Execution Environment:** Always assume tasks are run inside the activated virtual environment (`(venv)`). Do this:\n\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\n\nCreate config:\n\ncat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'\n{\n  \"project_id\": \"ctoteam\",\n  \"location\": \"us-central1\",\n  \"prompt_id\": \"3364082818773680128\",\n  \"model\": \"gemini-3.5-flash\"\n}\nEOF\n\nCreate reader script:\n\ncat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'\nimport json, subprocess, requests\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\ncfg = json.load(open(\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\"))\nproject = cfg[\"project_id\"]\nlocation = cfg[\"location\"]\nprompt_id = cfg[\"prompt_id\"]\n\ntoken = subprocess.check_output(\n    [\"gcloud\", \"auth\", \"print-access-token\"], text=True\n).strip()\n\nheaders = {\n    \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {token}\",\n    \"x-goog-user-project\": project,\n    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n}\n\ncandidate_urls = [\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/prompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}\",\n    f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1beta1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/savedPrompts/{prompt_id}\",\n]\n\nPath(\"saved_prompts\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\nfor url in candidate_urls:\n    print(f\"\\nTRY: {url}\")\n    r = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)\n    print(\"STATUS:\", r.status_code)\n\n    if r.status_code == 200:\n        data = r.json()\n        out = f\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\"\n        Path(out).write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\n        print(f\"SUCCESS saved to {out}\")\n        print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:3000])\n        break\n    else:\n        print(r.text[:1000])\nelse:\n    print(\"\\nFAILED: No API endpoint worked. Need SDK/API name verification.\")\nEOF\n\nRun:\n\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\n\nAlso verify auth:\n\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam\n\nIf this works, next step is:\n\nsaved_prompts/raw_3364082818773680128.json\n\nThen we wire it into:\n\n./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\n\nKey point: we read by prompt_id 3364082818773680128, not by the Console URL.\n"

                  "thought": true
                },
                {
                  "text": "The issue you ran into is due to two distinct system/authentication limits:\n1. **PEP 668 Restriction (`externally-managed-environment`):** Modern Python installations (like Python 3.12 on your VM) restrict installing global packages using `pip` without virtual environments.\n2. **Expired `gcloud` Credentials:** The traceback from your python script occurred because `gcloud auth print-access-token` exited with status 1 due to expired authentication.\n\n### The \"Under-the-Hood\" Solution\nIn Google Cloud, Vertex AI Studio prompt management is structurally backed by the **Vertex AI Dataset API** under the hood. When you save a prompt in Vertex AI Studio, Google creates a standard Dataset resource located at:\n`projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}`\n\nBy querying this REST API directly, you get direct access to the raw JSON of your saved prompt. This is extremely robust and **bypasses any strict Python SDK protobuf parser version conflicts** (which often crash on older Python client setups due to newer features in saved prompt models).\n\n---\n\n### Step-by-Step Recovery Guide\n\n#### Step 1: Fix `gcloud` Authentication\nTo clear the expired auth on your remote VM, run the headless login command:\n```bash\ngcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\n```\nCopy and paste the URL into your browser, grant permissions, and paste the verification code back into your terminal.\n\nNext, set up the project and quota project targets:\n```bash\ngcloud config set project ctoteam\ngcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\ngcloud services enable aiplatform.googleapis.com --project=ctoteam\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Install Python Libraries Safely\nYou can bypass the PEP 668 environment lock by adding the `--break-system-packages` flag, or by creating a clean virtual environment:\n\n**Option A (Quick command bypass):**\n```bash\npython3 -m pip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q --break-system-packages\n```\n\n**Option B (Clean virtual environment - Recommended):**\n```bash\npython3 -m venv venv\nsource venv/bin/activate\npip install requests google-cloud-aiplatform -q\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Write the Config\nCreate the workspace directories (if you haven't yet) and write the configurations:\n```bash\ncd /home/appadmin/projects/Ram_Projects/DiracDelta/gcloud_run\nmkdir -p agents configs saved_prompts logs\n```\n\n```bash\ncat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'\n{\n  \"project_id\": \"ctoteam\",\n  \"location\": \"us-central1\",\n  \"prompt_id\": \"3364082818773680128\",\n  \"model\": \"gemini-3.5-flash\"\n}\nEOF\n```\n\n#### Step 4: Write the Robust Dataset-based Reader Script\nThis script targets the standard underlying Vertex AI Dataset API endpoint, parses the prompt's structural schema, and outputs `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` in Markdown format:\n\n```bash\ncat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport requests\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# Load config\ntry:\n    cfg = json.load(open(\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\"))\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    print(\"Config file not found. Ensure configs/saved_prompt_config.json exists.\")\n    exit(1)\n\nproject = cfg[\"project_id\"]\nlocation = cfg[\"location\"]\nprompt_id = cfg[\"prompt_id\"]\n\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\ntry:\n    token = subprocess.check_output(\n        [\"gcloud\", \"auth\", \"print-access-token\"], text=True\n    ).strip()\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n    print(\"\\nERROR: Failed to retrieve access token from gcloud.\")\n    print(\"Please run the following in your terminal first to authenticate:\")\n    print(\"  gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\")\n    print(\"  gcloud config set project ctoteam\")\n    exit(1)\n\nheaders = {\n    \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {token}\",\n    \"x-goog-user-project\": project,\n    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n}\n\n# Vertex AI Studio Saved Prompts are structurally managed as Dataset resources\nurl = f\"https://{location}-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/datasets/{prompt_id}\"\n\nprint(f\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST endpoint:\\n{url}\\n\")\nr = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)\n\nprint(\"Status Code:\", r.status_code)\n\nif r.status_code != 200:\n    print(\"Error details:\")\n    print(r.text)\n    print(\"\\nIf you see a permission error, ensure you ran:\")\n    print(\"  gcloud auth application-default set-quota-project ctoteam\")\n    exit(1)\n\ndata = r.json()\n\n# Ensure destination exists\nPath(\"saved_prompts\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n\n# Save the raw backend representation JSON\nPath(f\"saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\nprint(f\"Raw prompt metadata saved to: saved_prompts/raw_{prompt_id}.json\")\n\n# Safely extract System Instructions and Prompt Text out of the structural schema\nmetadata = data.get(\"metadata\", {})\nprompt_api_schema = metadata.get(\"prompt_api_schema\", {})\nprompt_message = prompt_api_schema.get(\"prompt_message\", {})\n\n# 1. Extract System Instructions\nsystem_instruction_parts = prompt_message.get(\"systemInstruction\", {}).get(\"parts\", [])\nsystem_instruction_text = \"\\n\".join([part.get(\"text\", \"\") for part in system_instruction_parts if \"text\" in part])\n\n# 2. Extract User Prompt Text\ncontents = prompt_message.get(\"contents\", [])\nuser_prompt_parts = []\nfor content in contents:\n    if content.get(\"role\") == \"user\" or not content.get(\"role\"):\n        for part in content.get(\"parts\", []):\n            if \"text\" in part:\n                user_prompt_parts.append(part[\"text\"])\nuser_prompt_text = \"\\n\".join(user_prompt_parts)\n\n# Construct the assembled Markdown file\nassembled_md_content = f\"\"\"# System Instructions\n{system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \"(None provided)\"}\n\n# User Prompt\n{user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \"(None provided)\"}\n\"\"\"\n\nPath(\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\nprint(\"Assembled prompt written successfully to: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\")\n\nprint(\"\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\")\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1500])\nif len(assembled_md_content) > 1500:\n    print(\"\\n... [truncated for preview]\")\nprint(\"----------------------\")\nEOF\n```\n\n#### Step 5: Run and Verify\nExecute your reader:\n```bash\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\n```\n\nOnce it returns a successful preview, launch aider using your newly read prompt:\n```bash\n./start_aider.sh gemini-flash saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\n```",

`2114685765899255808`.\n\nHere is the clean, robust fix to update both your configuration and the Python script safely.\n\n### Step 1: Update the Prompt ID Config\nRun this in your terminal to set your new prompt ID `2114685765899255808`:\n```bash\ncat > configs/saved_prompt_config.json <<'EOF'\n{\n  \"project_id\": \"ctoteam\",\n  \"location\": \"us-central1\",\n  \"prompt_id\": \"2114685765899255808\",\n  \"model\": \"gemini-3.5-flash\"\n}\nEOF\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Overwrite the Reader Script (Syntax-Proofed)\nTo guarantee your terminal clipboard does not mangle the code, the script below **does not use f-string curly braces** for the token headers (avoiding the syntax error), and has been streamlined for clean terminal pasting.\n\nRun this command in your VM terminal:\n```bash\ncat > agents/read_saved_prompt.py <<'EOF'\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport requests\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\n# Load configuration\ntry:\n    cfg = json.load(open(\"configs/saved_prompt_config.json\"))\nexcept FileNotFoundError:\n    print(\"Config file configs/saved_prompt_config.json not found.\")\n    exit(1)\n\nproject = cfg[\"project_id\"]\nlocation = cfg[\"location\"]\nprompt_id = cfg[\"prompt_id\"]\n\n# Retrieve authentication token from gcloud\ntry:\n    token = subprocess.check_output(\n        [\"gcloud\", \"auth\", \"print-access-token\"], text=True\n    ).strip()\nexcept subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n    print(\"\\nERROR: gcloud token retrieval failed.\")\n    print(\"Please run: gcloud auth login --no-launch-browser\")\n    exit(1)\n\nheaders = {\n    \"Authorization\": \"Bearer \" + token,\n    \"x-goog-user-project\": project,\n    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n}\n\n# The Vertex AI / Agent Platform dataset endpoint\nurl = \"https://\" + location + \"-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/\" + project + \"/locations/\" + location + \"/datasets/\" + prompt_id\n\nprint(\"Fetching saved prompt dataset from REST API...\")\nr = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=30)\nprint(\"Status Code:\", r.status_code)\n\nif r.status_code != 200:\n    print(\"Error details:\", r.text)\n    exit(1)\n\ndata = r.json()\nPath(\"saved_prompts\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\nPath(\"saved_prompts/raw_\" + prompt_id + \".json\").write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))\nprint(\"Saved raw metadata to saved_prompts/raw_\" + prompt_id + \".json\")\n\nmetadata = data.get(\"metadata\", {})\nprompt_api_schema = metadata.get(\"prompt_api_schema\", {})\nprompt_message = prompt_api_schema.get(\"prompt_message\", {})\n\n# Extract System Instructions\nsys_parts = prompt_message.get(\"systemInstruction\", {}).get(\"parts\", [])\nsystem_instruction_text = \"\\n\".join([p.get(\"text\", \"\") for p in sys_parts if \"text\" in p])\n\n# Extract User Prompt\ncontents = prompt_message.get(\"contents\", [])\nuser_prompt_parts = []\nfor content in contents:\n    if content.get(\"role\") == \"user\" or not content.get(\"role\"):\n        for p in content.get(\"parts\", []):\n            if \"text\" in p:\n                user_prompt_parts.append(p[\"text\"])\nuser_prompt_text = \"\\n\".join(user_prompt_parts)\n\nassembled_md_content = \"\"\"# System Instructions\n\"\"\" + (system_instruction_text if system_instruction_text else \"(None)\") + \"\"\"\n\n# User Prompt\n\"\"\" + (user_prompt_text if user_prompt_text else \"(None)\") + \"\\n\"\n\nPath(\"saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\").write_text(assembled_md_content)\nprint(\"Successfully generated: saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md\")\nprint(\"\\n--- PROMPT PREVIEW ---\")\nprint(assembled_md_content[:1000])\nprint(\"----------------------\")\nEOF\n```\n\n---\n\n### Step 3: Run the Script\nNow execute the script inside your active virtual environment:\n```bash\npython3 agents/read_saved_prompt.py\n```\n\nIt should run cleanly, outputting `saved_prompts/assembled_prompt.md` containing your newly active Studio prompt (`2114685765899255808`).",

                  "text": "**Analyzing API Response**\n\nI'm now examining the raw JSON returned from the REST API.
